## Mode: api-alignment

## Context

This is the corrective for a STUCK route (progress-critic ts229): a 10-iter-flat
`SheafOfModules`-over-a-scheme substrate whose cost estimates keep being generated by
inspection and then falsified by the prover. We are about to dispatch a prover at the
**A-engine** and want to verify the Mathlib idiom BEFORE the prover discovers a gap
mid-build (which is exactly what happened to the sibling C-bridge at iter-228).

## Proposed declaration / design under review

We need to build, in the project file `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean`:

```
AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.homOfLocalCompat :
  {X : Scheme} {M N : X.Modules} {ι : Type} (U : ι → Opens X) (hU : ⨆ i, U i = ⊤)
  (f : Π i, M.restrict (U i).ι ⟶ N.restrict (U i).ι)
  (compat : ∀ i j, ... agree on overlaps ...)  →  (M ⟶ N)
```

i.e. **glue a family of compatible local morphisms of sheaves-of-modules (one per open
`Uᵢ` of a cover) into a single global morphism `M ⟶ N`** in `X.Modules =
SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf`. This is the "morphism descent" A-bridge of a larger
⊗-inverse construction.

The project's intended route (by inspection, ~120–190 LOC, UNVERIFIED):
1. Form the internal-hom **presheaf of types** `presheafHom (M.val.presheaf)
   (N.val.presheaf)` and note it is a `TopCat.Sheaf` of types via `Presheaf.IsSheaf.hom`
   (needs `N.val` a sheaf).
2. Each local `fᵢ : M.restrict (Uᵢ).ι ⟶ N.restrict (Uᵢ).ι` yields a **section**
   `localSection i : presheafHom(...).obj (op Uᵢ)`. Its component at `(V, h : V ⟶ Uᵢ)` is
   an `eqToHom`-conjugated `fᵢ.mapPresheaf` component using `(Uᵢ).ι ''ᵁ ((Uᵢ).ι ⁻¹ᵁ V) = V`
   for `V ≤ Uᵢ`. **The `naturality` field of `localSection` is the load-bearing coherence
   step** flagged as the only real risk.
3. Glue via `TopCat.Sheaf.existsUnique_gluing` (the `localSection`s agree on overlaps by
   `compat`) to a global section = an ab-sheaf morphism `M.val.presheaf ⟶ N.val.presheaf`.
4. Re-attach 𝒪_X-linearity via the already-built `Scheme.Modules.homMk` (A-bridge step ii)
   to land the morphism in `X.Modules`.

## Questions (api-alignment)

1. **Does Mathlib already provide morphism-gluing for `SheafOfModules` / `PresheafOfModules`
   over a ringed space** — i.e. a `homOfLocalCompat`-shaped lemma or a hom-sheaf whose
   `existsUnique_gluing` gives exactly "compatible local module-morphisms glue to a global
   one"? Check `Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Sheaf.lean`,
   `Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Presheaf/*`, `CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafHom.lean`,
   `CategoryTheory/Sites/Sheafification`. If Mathlib has it, we should consume it, not
   re-build — name the exact declaration and the cost of the parallel build.

2. **Is `TopCat.Sheaf.existsUnique_gluing` on `presheafHom (…) (…)`-as-`TopCat.Sheaf` the
   right idiom**, or does Mathlib prefer `CategoryTheory.Sites.SheafHom` /
   `sheafHomSectionsEquiv` / `Sheaf.homEquiv` / a `yoneda`+`Sheaf`-hom adjunction route for
   "a global morphism = a global section of the internal-hom sheaf"? Which is less LOC and
   less coherence-proof-obligation?

3. **The `localSection` `naturality` field**: is the `eqToHom`-conjugated `mapPresheaf`-
   component construction (transporting along `(Uᵢ).ι ''ᵁ ((Uᵢ).ι ⁻¹ᵁ V) = V`) the
   Mathlib-idiomatic way to turn a `restrict`-along-open-immersion morphism into a section of
   the hom-presheaf over `Uᵢ`? Is there a Mathlib helper (`PresheafOfModules.restrict`
   adjunction unit/counit, `Opens.map`/`Opens.openEmbedding` restriction API,
   `restrictFunctor` naturality) that discharges this naturality WITHOUT a hand `eqToHom`
   juggle? The C-bridge died on a comparable slice/`Over`-reindexing coherence — we want to
   know if THIS coherence is the same class of Mathlib-absent gap or a discharged idiom.

## Deliverable

State plainly whether Mathlib already does this (PROCEED-by-reuse), whether the proposed
route is the right idiom (PROCEED), or whether the `localSection` naturality is another
Mathlib-absent coherence gap of the same kind that killed the C-bridge (ALIGN / GAP — with
the LOC the bespoke build would actually cost). A frank "this naturality is a real gap, ~N
LOC" is high-value — it is the data the USER needs for the ROUTE-C PAUSE decision.

But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. 10	# The `Scheme.Modules.tensorObj` substrate (A.1.c.SubT)
11	
12	This file is the **A.1.c.SubT** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the
13	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It records the dedicated
14	substrate on which the abelian-group instance of the relative Picard quotient
15	`Pic^♯_{C/k}(T) := Pic(C ×_k T) / π_T^* Pic(T)` rests (the residual `sorry`
16	of `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/RelPicFunctor.lean`).
17	
18	The mathematics is straightforward; the obstacle to formalisation is purely
19	infrastructural. The Lean construction of the abelian-group law
20	`[L] + [L'] := [L ⊗ L']` on isomorphism classes of line bundles requires three
21	ingredients on the Lean carrier:
22	
23	1. a binary tensor-product operation
24	   `⊗ : Scheme.Modules X × Scheme.Modules X → Scheme.Modules X`;
25	2. the structure sheaf `O_X` as a designated unit object for `⊗`;
26	3. an inverse operation on the full subcategory of invertible objects, i.e.
27	   the dual `L⁻¹ = Hom(L, O_X)` of an invertible sheaf.
28	
29	At Mathlib's pinned commit (`b80f227`), only a presheaf-level version of (1)
30	is available (`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj`); (2) and (3) are present
31	as scheme-level objects, but the binary operation in (1) that ties them
32	together at the `Scheme.Modules` level is missing, and there is no
33	`MonoidalCategory` instance on `Scheme.Modules X`. This file records the
34	project-side substrate that supplies (1) and consequently lifts (2) + (3)
35	into a monoidal-category structure on `Scheme.Modules X`.
36	
37	## Status (current)
38	
39	`tensorObj` and `tensorObj_functoriality` are fully defined (no `sorry`), lifting
40	`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` through sheafification on the small Zariski
41	site. The remaining typed-`sorry` residuals are the `⊗`-inverse lane
42	(`exists_tensorObj_inverse`, `addCommGroup_via_tensorObj`) and the route-(e)
43	whiskering residual `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W`. The dual-block is now
44	complete axiom-clean: the value layer (`InternalHom.internalHom`, `dual`,
45	`evalLin`/`internalHomEvalApp`) plus the evaluation morphism `internalHomEval`
46	(its naturality CLOSED iter-224, see below). Once the inverse lands, the consumer
47	`PicSharp.addCommGroup_via_tensorObj` closes the residual `addCommGroup`
48	sorry of `RelPicFunctor.lean` L235.

51	heartbeat-bomb diagnosis (the codomain `𝟙_` forcing `kabstract` to whnf the monoidal-unit
52	machinery on the first rewrite) was STALE — a Mathlib update made the composition split cleanly
53	with `erw [ModuleCat.hom_comp, …]`, after which the six-step `evalLin`/`naturality_apply`/`hdt`
54	reduction goes through with no bomb, no `with_reducible`, and no `maxHeartbeats` bump.

56	The 3 blueprint-pinned declarations are:
57	
58	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj` (def) — the substrate binary
59	   operation `⊗ : Scheme.Modules X × Scheme.Modules X → Scheme.Modules X`,
60	   lifting `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` on underlying presheaves
61	   composed with sheafification on the small Zariski site.
62	   Per blueprint `def:scheme_modules_tensorobj`.
63

65	   functorial action of `⊗` on morphisms: a pair `f : M ⟶ M'`, `g : N ⟶ N'`
66	   determines `f ⊗ g : tensorObj M N ⟶ tensorObj M' N'`.
67	   Per blueprint `lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality`.

72	isomorphisms, never a coherent monoidal category, so no such instance is on the

76	   `AddCommGroup` structure on the relative Picard quotient
77	   `Quotient (RelPicPresheaf.preimage_subgroup πC πT)`, built via the

103	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — base change along a ring iso commutes
104	with `⊗` (the H2 "bottom gap" of `tensorObj_restrict_iso`)
105	
106	For a *ring isomorphism* `e : R ≃+* S` and `S`-modules `A`, `B`, base change along
107	`e` (giving each `S`-module its `R`-module structure via `Module.compHom _ e.toRingHom`)
108	commutes with the tensor product: the canonical map `a ⊗ₜ[R] b ↦ a ⊗ₜ[S] b` is an
109	`R`-linear equivalence `A ⊗[R] B ≃ₗ[R] A ⊗[S] B`. Equivalently, `restrictScalars`
110	along a ring iso is *strong* monoidal — the lax tensorator is invertible. Mathlib
111	has `ModuleCat.extendScalars` strong monoidal but `restrictScalars` only
112	`LaxMonoidal`; this ring-iso strong upgrade is absent and is the documented "REAL
113	bottom gap" (H2) of `tensorObj_restrict_iso`. -/
114	
115	section RestrictScalarsRingIsoTensor
116	
117	open TensorProduct
118	
119	variable {R S : Type u} [CommRing R] [CommRing S]
120	
121	/-- The `R`-linear equivalence `A ⊗[R] B ≃ₗ[R] A ⊗[S] B` (`a ⊗ₜ b ↦ a ⊗ₜ b`),
122	where the `R`-module structures are base-changed along the ring iso `e : R ≃+* S`.
123	Base change along a ring iso commutes with `⊗`. -/
124	noncomputable def restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv (e : R ≃+* S)
125	    (A B : Type u) [AddCommGroup A] [Module S A] [AddCommGroup B] [Module S B] :
126	    letI _iA : Module R A := Module.compHom A e.toRingHom
127	    letI _iB : Module R B := Module.compHom B e.toRingHom
128	    letI _iT : Module R (TensorProduct S A B) := Module.compHom _ e.toRingHom
129	    TensorProduct R A B ≃ₗ[R] TensorProduct S A B := by
130	  letI _iA : Module R A := Module.compHom A e.toRingHom
131	  letI _iB : Module R B := Module.compHom B e.toRingHom
132	  letI _iT : Module R (TensorProduct S A B) := Module.compHom _ e.toRingHom
133	  -- Forward: `a ⊗ₜ[R] b ↦ a ⊗ₜ[S] b`, an `R`-bilinear-to-linear lift.
134	  let fwd : TensorProduct R A B →ₗ[R] TensorProduct S A B :=
135	    TensorProduct.lift
136	      { toFun := fun a =>
137	          { toFun := fun b => a ⊗ₜ[S] b
138	            map_add' := fun b b' => by rw [TensorProduct.tmul_add]
139	            map_smul' := fun r b => by
140	              simp only [RingHom.id_apply]
141	              change a ⊗ₜ[S] (e r • b) = e r • (a ⊗ₜ[S] b)
142	              rw [TensorProduct.tmul_smul] }
143	        map_add' := fun a a' => by ext b; simp [TensorProduct.add_tmul]
144	        map_smul' := fun r a => by
145	          ext b
146	          simp only [RingHom.id_apply, LinearMap.smul_apply, LinearMap.coe_mk,
147	            AddHom.coe_mk]
148	          change (e r • a) ⊗ₜ[S] b = e r • (a ⊗ₜ[S] b)
149	          rw [TensorProduct.smul_tmul', TensorProduct.smul_tmul] }
150	  -- Inverse: `a ⊗ₜ[S] b ↦ a ⊗ₜ[R] b`. Built as an additive lift out of the
151	  -- `S`-tensor (scalar-swap compatibility uses `s • a = e.symm s •ᵣ a`), then
152	  -- shown `R`-linear (`R` acting on the `S`-tensor via `e`).
153	  let bwdAdd : TensorProduct S A B →+ TensorProduct R A B :=
154	    TensorProduct.liftAddHom
155	      { toFun := fun a =>
156	          { toFun := fun b => a ⊗ₜ[R] b
157	            map_zero' := by rw [TensorProduct.tmul_zero]
158	            map_add' := fun b b' => by rw [TensorProduct.tmul_add] }
159	        map_zero' := by ext b; simp [TensorProduct.zero_tmul]
160	        map_add' := fun a a' => by ext b; simp [TensorProduct.add_tmul] }
161	      (fun s a b => by
162	        simp only [AddMonoidHom.coe_mk, ZeroHom.coe_mk]
163	        -- `(s • a) ⊗ₜ[R] b = a ⊗ₜ[R] (s • b)`; move the `S`-scalar through `e.symm`.
164	        have hsa : (s • a) = (e.symm s : R) • a := by
165	          change s • a = e (e.symm s) • a; rw [e.apply_symm_apply]
166	        have hsb : (s • b) = (e.symm s : R) • b := by
167	          change s • b = e (e.symm s) • b; rw [e.apply_symm_apply]
168	        rw [hsa, hsb]; exact TensorProduct.smul_tmul _ _ _ )
169	  let bwd : TensorProduct S A B →ₗ[R] TensorProduct R A B :=
170	    { toFun := bwdAdd
171	      map_add' := bwdAdd.map_add
172	      map_smul' := fun r x => by
173	        simp only [RingHom.id_apply]
174	        -- `R` acts on the `S`-tensor via `e`; reduce to additive `S`-scalar action.
175	        change bwdAdd (e r • x) = r • bwdAdd x
176	        induction x using TensorProduct.induction_on with
177	        | zero => simp
178	        | tmul a b =>
179	            rw [TensorProduct.smul_tmul']
180	            change (e r • a) ⊗ₜ[R] b = r • (a ⊗ₜ[R] b)
181	            rw [TensorProduct.smul_tmul']
182	            rfl
183	        | add x y hx hy =>
184	            rw [smul_add, map_add, map_add, hx, hy, smul_add] }
185	  refine LinearEquiv.ofLinear fwd bwd ?_ ?_
186	  · -- right inverse `fwd ∘ bwd = id`. The composite is `R`-linear over the
187	    -- `S`-tensor, so check on additive generators by induction.
188	    refine LinearMap.ext fun x => ?_
189	    simp only [LinearMap.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply, LinearMap.id_coe, id_eq]
190	    induction x using TensorProduct.induction_on with
191	    | zero => simp
192	    | tmul a b =>
193	        change fwd (bwdAdd (a ⊗ₜ[S] b)) = a ⊗ₜ[S] b
194	        change fwd (a ⊗ₜ[R] b) = a ⊗ₜ[S] b
195	        simp only [fwd, TensorProduct.lift.tmul, LinearMap.coe_mk, AddHom.coe_mk]
196	    | add x y hx hy => rw [map_add bwd, map_add fwd, hx, hy]
197	  · -- left inverse `bwd ∘ fwd = id` on `a ⊗ₜ[R] b` (composite `R`-linear over the
198	    -- `R`-tensor, so `TensorProduct.ext'` applies).
199	    refine TensorProduct.ext' fun a b => ?_
200	    change bwdAdd (fwd (a ⊗ₜ[R] b)) = a ⊗ₜ[R] b
201	    change bwdAdd (a ⊗ₜ[S] b) = a ⊗ₜ[R] b
202	    rfl
203	
204	/-- The forward map of `restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv` on a simple tensor:
205	`a ⊗ₜ[R] b ↦ a ⊗ₜ[S] b`. -/
206	@[simp] lemma restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv_apply_tmul (e : R ≃+* S)
207	    (A B : Type u) [AddCommGroup A] [Module S A] [AddCommGroup B] [Module S B] (a : A) (b : B) :
208	    letI _iA : Module R A := Module.compHom A e.toRingHom
209	    letI _iB : Module R B := Module.compHom B e.toRingHom
210	    letI _iT : Module R (TensorProduct S A B) := Module.compHom _ e.toRingHom
211	    restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv e A B (a ⊗ₜ[R] b) = a ⊗ₜ[S] b := by
212	  letI _iA : Module R A := Module.compHom A e.toRingHom
213	  letI _iB : Module R B := Module.compHom B e.toRingHom
214	  letI _iT : Module R (TensorProduct S A B) := Module.compHom _ e.toRingHom
215	  simp only [restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv, LinearEquiv.ofLinear_apply,
216	    TensorProduct.lift.tmul, LinearMap.coe_mk, AddHom.coe_mk]
217	
218	/-- **`ModuleCat.restrictScalars` along a ring isomorphism is strong monoidal: the
219	lax tensorator `μ` is an isomorphism.** This is the "REAL bottom gap" (H2) of
220	`tensorObj_restrict_iso`. For a ring iso `e : R ≃+* S` and `S`-modules `M₁, M₂`,
221	the underlying map of the lax tensorator
222	`μ : restrictScalars(M₁) ⊗_R restrictScalars(M₂) ⟶ restrictScalars(M₁ ⊗_S M₂)`
223	sends `m₁ ⊗ₜ m₂ ↦ m₁ ⊗ₜ m₂` (`ModuleCat.restrictScalars_μ_tmul`), which is exactly
224	the forward `R`-linear equivalence `restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv e`, hence is
225	bijective, hence an isomorphism. Mathlib has `extendScalars` strong monoidal but
226	only `restrictScalars` lax; this ring-iso strong upgrade is the documented absent
227	ingredient. -/
228	lemma restrictScalars_isIso_μ (e : R ≃+* S) (M₁ M₂ : ModuleCat.{u} S) :
229	    IsIso (Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars e.toRingHom) M₁ M₂) := by
230	  rw [ConcreteCategory.isIso_iff_bijective]
231	  have hfun : ⇑(Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars e.toRingHom) M₁ M₂)
232	      = ⇑(restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv e M₁ M₂) := by
233	    ext z
234	    induction z using TensorProduct.induction_on with
235	    | zero => rw [map_zero]; exact (map_zero _).symm
236	    | tmul a b =>
237	        erw [ModuleCat.restrictScalars_μ_tmul]
238	        exact (restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv_apply_tmul e M₁ M₂ a b).symm
239	    | add x y hx hy => rw [map_add, hx, hy]; exact (map_add _ x y).symm
240	  rw [hfun]
241	  exact (restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv e M₁ M₂).bijective
242	
243	/-- **The lax-monoidal unit `ε` of `restrictScalars` along a ring iso is an
244	isomorphism.** Its underlying map is the ring map `e` (`ModuleCat.restrictScalars_η`),
245	which is bijective since `e` is a ring equivalence. -/
246	lemma restrictScalars_isIso_ε (e : R ≃+* S) :
247	    IsIso (Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε (ModuleCat.restrictScalars e.toRingHom)) := by
248	  rw [ConcreteCategory.isIso_iff_bijective]
249	  have hfun : ⇑(Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε (ModuleCat.restrictScalars e.toRingHom)) = ⇑e := by
250	    ext r
251	    exact ModuleCat.restrictScalars_η (f := e.toRingHom) r
252	  rw [hfun]
253	  exact e.bijective
254	
255	/-- **`ModuleCat.restrictScalars` along a ring isomorphism is (strong) monoidal.**
256	The packaged `Functor.Monoidal` structure obtained from the lax structure by
257	inverting `ε` (`restrictScalars_isIso_ε`) and `μ` (`restrictScalars_isIso_μ`). This
258	is the clean, reusable strong-monoidal upgrade that Mathlib provides for
259	`extendScalars` but not `restrictScalars`; it is the ModuleCat-level core of the H2
260	ingredient of `tensorObj_restrict_iso`. -/
261	@[implicit_reducible]
262	noncomputable def restrictScalarsMonoidalOfRingEquiv (e : R ≃+* S) :
263	    (ModuleCat.restrictScalars e.toRingHom).Monoidal := by
264	  haveI : IsIso (Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε (ModuleCat.restrictScalars e.toRingHom)) :=
265	    restrictScalars_isIso_ε e
266	  haveI : ∀ M₁ M₂, IsIso (Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars e.toRingHom) M₁ M₂) :=
267	    fun M₁ M₂ => restrictScalars_isIso_μ e M₁ M₂
268	  exact Functor.Monoidal.ofLaxMonoidal _
269	
270	/-- **Bijective-ring-hom form of the strong-monoidal tensorator.** For an arbitrary
271	*bijective* ring hom `f : R →+* S`, the lax tensorator of `ModuleCat.restrictScalars f`
272	is an isomorphism. This is the form consumed by the presheaf-level lift, where the
273	componentwise ring map `(α.app X).hom` of a `NatIso` of ring presheaves is bijective
274	but not literally presented as `(_ : R ≃+* S).toRingHom`. -/
275	lemma restrictScalars_isIso_μ_of_bijective (f : R →+* S) (hf : Function.Bijective f)
276	    (M₁ M₂ : ModuleCat.{u} S) :
277	    IsIso (Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars f) M₁ M₂) := by
278	  have hfe : f = (RingEquiv.ofBijective f hf).toRingHom := RingHom.ext fun _ => rfl
279	  rw [hfe]
280	  exact restrictScalars_isIso_μ (RingEquiv.ofBijective f hf) M₁ M₂
281	
282	/-- **Bijective-ring-hom form of the strong-monoidal unit.** Companion of
283	`restrictScalars_isIso_μ_of_bijective` for the lax unit `ε`. -/
284	lemma restrictScalars_isIso_ε_of_bijective (f : R →+* S) (hf : Function.Bijective f) :
285	    IsIso (Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε (ModuleCat.restrictScalars f)) := by
286	  have hfe : f = (RingEquiv.ofBijective f hf).toRingHom := RingHom.ext fun _ => rfl
287	  rw [hfe]
288	  exact restrictScalars_isIso_ε (RingEquiv.ofBijective f hf)
289	
290	/-- **Base change along a ring iso commutes with the linear dual** (the `Hom`-analogue
291	of `restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv`). For a ring isomorphism `e : R ≃+* S` and an
292	`S`-module `M`, the `R`-linear equivalence
293	`restrictScalars_e (M →ₗ[S] S) ≃ₗ[R] (restrictScalars_e M →ₗ[R] R)`, `φ ↦ e.symm ∘ φ`.
294	
295	This is the ModuleCat-level **H2′ ingredient** of the (still-open, d.2-FREE) C-bridge
296	`(dual M).restrict f ≅ dual (M.restrict f)` along an open immersion `f`: the C-bridge
297	mirrors `tensorObj_restrict_iso` exactly (Step 1 `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`, Step 2
298	`sheafificationCompPullback`, Step 3 strip sheafification, Step 4 H1
299	`pushforwardPushforwardAdj`∘`leftAdjointUniq` ∘ this H2′), with `⊗` replaced by `dual`
300	and `restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv` replaced by this equivalence. Like its tensor
301	counterpart it is **pure algebra over a ring iso — no stalk, no filtered-colimit, no
302	whiskering of the sheafification unit (no d.2)**: a map `M → S` is `R`-linear (via `e`)
303	iff it is `S`-linear, and `e.symm` carries the codomain `S` to `R`. The forward map is
304	`R`-linear; the inverse `ψ ↦ e ∘ ψ` is its two-sided inverse. The `iMS` `Module R`
305	instance on `M →ₗ[S] S` is base change along `e`. -/
306	noncomputable def restrictScalarsRingIsoDualEquiv (e : R ≃+* S)
307	    (M : Type u) [AddCommGroup M] [Module S M] :
308	    letI _iM : Module R M := Module.compHom M e.toRingHom
309	    letI _iMS : Module R (M →ₗ[S] S) := Module.compHom _ e.toRingHom
310	    (M →ₗ[S] S) ≃ₗ[R] (M →ₗ[R] R) := by
311	  letI _iM : Module R M := Module.compHom M e.toRingHom
312	  letI _iMS : Module R (M →ₗ[S] S) := Module.compHom _ e.toRingHom
313	  have hsmulM : ∀ (r : R) (m : M), r • m = e r • m := fun _ _ => rfl
314	  refine
315	    { toFun := fun φ =>
316	        { toFun := fun m => e.symm (φ m)
317	          map_add' := fun m m' => by simp [map_add]
318	          map_smul' := fun r m => by
319	            simp only [RingHom.id_apply, smul_eq_mul]
320	            rw [hsmulM, map_smul, smul_eq_mul, map_mul, e.symm_apply_apply] }
321	      invFun := fun ψ =>
322	        { toFun := fun m => e (ψ m)
323	          map_add' := fun m m' => by simp [map_add]
324	          map_smul' := fun s m => by
325	            simp only [RingHom.id_apply, smul_eq_mul]
326	            have hsm : s • m = (e.symm s) • m := by rw [hsmulM, e.apply_symm_apply]
327	            rw [hsm, map_smul, smul_eq_mul, map_mul, e.apply_symm_apply] }
328	      left_inv := fun φ => by ext m; exact e.apply_symm_apply _
329	      right_inv := fun ψ => by ext m; exact e.symm_apply_apply _
330	      map_add' := fun φ φ' => by
331	        ext m
332	        simp only [LinearMap.add_apply, LinearMap.coe_mk, AddHom.coe_mk, map_add]
333	      map_smul' := fun r φ => by
334	        ext m
335	        simp only [RingHom.id_apply, LinearMap.smul_apply, LinearMap.coe_mk, AddHom.coe_mk,
336	          smul_eq_mul]
337	        change e.symm ((e r • φ) m) = r * e.symm (φ m)
338	        rw [LinearMap.smul_apply, smul_eq_mul, map_mul, e.symm_apply_apply] }
339	
340	end RestrictScalarsRingIsoTensor

342	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — `restrictScalars` is lax monoidal
343	
344	The presheaf-of-modules restriction-of-scalars functor along a morphism of
345	presheaves of *commutative* rings is lax monoidal. Mathlib ships the sectionwise
346	fact `ModuleCat.restrictScalars f` is `LaxMonoidal`
347	(`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Monoidal.Adjunction`); here we lift it to
348	the presheaf level through the sectionwise presheaf monoidal structure
349	(`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal`). This is the sole project-side ingredient feeding
350	the oplax comparison `δ` of `pullback φ` (the mate of `pushforward φ`) used to
351	close `tensorObj_restrict_iso`. Per blueprint `lem:restrictscalars_laxmonoidal`. -/
352	
353	namespace PresheafOfModules
354	
355	universe v'
356	
357	variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v'} C] {R S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
358	
359	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
360	/-- The lax-monoidal unit `ε` of `restrictScalars α`, assembled sectionwise from
361	`ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app X)`'s lax-monoidal unit. -/
362	noncomputable def restrictScalarsLaxε
363	    (α : R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat ⟶ S ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :
364	    (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))) ⟶
365	      (restrictScalars α).obj (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (S ⋙ forget₂ _ _))) where
366	  app X := Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app X).hom)
367	  naturality {X Y} f := by
368	    ext r
369	    dsimp
370	    erw [PresheafOfModules.unit_map_one, ModuleCat.restrictScalars_η,
371	      ModuleCat.restrictScalars_η]
372	    simp only [map_one]
373	    erw [PresheafOfModules.unit_map_one]
374	
375	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
376	/-- The lax-monoidal tensorator `μ` of `restrictScalars α`, assembled sectionwise
377	from `ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app X)`'s lax-monoidal tensorator. -/
378	noncomputable def restrictScalarsLaxμ
379	    (α : R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat ⟶ S ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
380	    (M₁ M₂ : PresheafOfModules.{u} (S ⋙ forget₂ _ _)) :
381	    (restrictScalars α).obj M₁ ⊗ (restrictScalars α).obj M₂ ⟶
382	      (restrictScalars α).obj (M₁ ⊗ M₂) where
383	  app X := by
384	    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app X).hom) (M₁.obj X) (M₂.obj X)
385	  naturality {X Y} f := by
386	    refine ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensor_ext (fun m₁ m₂ ↦ ?_)
387	    dsimp
388	    erw [PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_map_tmul, ModuleCat.restrictScalars_μ_tmul,
389	      ModuleCat.restrictScalars_μ_tmul, PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_map_tmul]
390	    rfl
391	
392	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
393	/-- **`restrictScalars α` is lax monoidal** for a morphism `α` of presheaves of
394	commutative rings. Project-local lift of `ModuleCat.instLaxMonoidalRestrictScalars`. -/
395	noncomputable instance restrictScalarsLaxMonoidal
396	    (α : R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat ⟶ S ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :
397	    (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).LaxMonoidal where
398	  ε := restrictScalarsLaxε α
399	  μ M₁ M₂ := restrictScalarsLaxμ α M₁ M₂
400	  μ_natural_left := by
401	    intro X Y f X'
402	    ext1 Z
403	    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ_natural_left (F := ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app Z).hom)
404	      (f.app Z) (X'.obj Z)
405	  μ_natural_right := by
406	    intro X Y X' f
407	    ext1 Z
408	    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ_natural_right (F := ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app Z).hom)
409	      (X'.obj Z) (f.app Z)
410	  associativity := by
411	    intro M N P
412	    ext1 Z
413	    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.associativity (F := ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app Z).hom)
414	      (M.obj Z) (N.obj Z) (P.obj Z)
415	  left_unitality := by
416	    intro M
417	    ext1 Z
418	    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.left_unitality (F := ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app Z).hom)
419	      (M.obj Z)
420	  right_unitality := by
421	    intro M
422	    ext1 Z
423	    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.right_unitality (F := ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app Z).hom)
424	      (M.obj Z)

426	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — flat left-whiskering preserves the
427	sheafification localizer
428	
429	The single non-formal ingredient of the `⊗`-invertibility associator
430	(`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_assoc_iso`, blueprint
431	`lem:flat_whisker_localizer`): for a sectionwise-*flat* presheaf of modules `F`
432	and a morphism `g` that is locally injective / locally surjective for the
433	Grothendieck topology `J` (i.e. lies in the sheafification localizer `J.W`), the
434	left-whiskered morphism `F ◁ g` is again locally injective / surjective. Local
435	surjectivity is pure right-exactness of `⊗` (no flatness); local injectivity is
436	where sectionwise flatness enters, via `Module.Flat.lTensor_exact`. All
437	ingredients are present in Mathlib — the route uses **no** `MonoidalClosed`
438	structure. -/
439	
440	section FlatWhisker
441	
442	open MonoidalCategory CategoryTheory.Presheaf
443

module

public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Over

/-! Internal hom of sheaves

In this file, given two sheaves `F` and `G` on a site `(C, J)` with values
in a category `A`, we define a sheaf of types
`sheafHom F G` which sends `X : C` to the type of morphisms
between the restrictions of `F` and `G` to the categories `Over X`.

We first define `presheafHom F G` when `F` and `G` are
presheaves `Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A` and show that it is a sheaf when `G` is a sheaf.

TODO:
- turn both `presheafHom` and `sheafHom` into bifunctors
- for a sheaf of types `F`, the `sheafHom` functor from `F` is right-adjoint to
  the product functor with `F`, i.e. for all `X` and `Y`, there is a
  natural bijection `(X ⨯ F ⟶ Y) ≃ (X ⟶ sheafHom F Y)`.
- use these results in order to show that the category of sheaves of types is Cartesian closed

-/

@[expose] public section

universe v v' u u'

namespace CategoryTheory

open Category Opposite Limits

variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
  {A : Type u'} [Category.{v'} A]

variable (F G : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A)

/-- Given two presheaves `F` and `G` on a category `C` with values in a category `A`,
this `presheafHom F G` is the presheaf of types which sends an object `X : C`
to the type of morphisms between the "restrictions" of `F` and `G` to the category `Over X`. -/
@[simps! obj]
def presheafHom : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ Type _ where
  obj X := (Over.forget X.unop).op ⋙ F ⟶ (Over.forget X.unop).op ⋙ G
  map f := TypeCat.ofHom (Functor.whiskerLeft (Over.map f.unop).op)
  map_id := by
    rintro ⟨X⟩
    ext φ ⟨Y⟩
    simpa [Over.mapId] using φ.naturality ((Over.mapId X).hom.app Y).op
  map_comp := by
    rintro ⟨X⟩ ⟨Y⟩ ⟨Z⟩ ⟨f : Y ⟶ X⟩ ⟨g : Z ⟶ Y⟩
    ext φ ⟨W⟩
    simpa [Over.mapComp] using φ.naturality ((Over.mapComp g f).hom.app W).op

variable {F G}

/-- Equational lemma for the presheaf structure on `presheafHom`.

open Presheaf CategoryTheory

section

variable [HasLimitsOfSize.{x, x} C] [(CategoryTheory.forget C).ReflectsIsomorphisms]
variable [PreservesLimitsOfSize.{x, x} (CategoryTheory.forget C)]

/-- A more convenient way of obtaining a unique gluing of sections for a sheaf.
-/
theorem existsUnique_gluing (sf : ∀ i : ι, ToType (F.1.obj (op (U i))))
    (h : IsCompatible F.1 U sf) :
    ∃! s : ToType (F.1.obj (op (iSup U))), IsGluing F.1 U sf s :=
  IsSheaf.isSheafUniqueGluing F.property U sf h

/-- In this version of the lemma, the inclusion homs `iUV` can be specified directly by the user,
which can be more convenient in practice.
-/
theorem existsUnique_gluing' (V : Opens X) (iUV : ∀ i : ι, U i ⟶ V) (hcover : V ≤ iSup U)
    (sf : ∀ i : ι, ToType (F.1.obj (op (U i)))) (h : IsCompatible F.1 U sf) :
    ∃! s : ToType (F.1.obj (op V)), ∀ i : ι, F.1.map (iUV i).op s = sf i := by
  have V_eq_supr_U : V = iSup U := le_antisymm hcover (iSup_le fun i => (iUV i).le)
  obtain ⟨gl, gl_spec, gl_uniq⟩ := F.existsUnique_gluing U sf h
  refine ⟨F.1.map (eqToHom V_eq_supr_U).op gl, ?_, ?_⟩
  · intro i

/-

module

public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Pushforward
public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf
public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Over
public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Comma.Over.Pullback
public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Limits.Shapes.BinaryProducts

/-!
# Pushforward of sheaves of modules

Assume that categories `C` and `D` are equipped with Grothendieck topologies, and
that `F : C ⥤ D` is a continuous functor.
Then, if `φ : S ⟶ (F.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).obj R` is
a morphism of sheaves of rings, we construct the pushforward functor
`pushforward φ : SheafOfModules.{v} R ⥤ SheafOfModules.{v} S`, and
we show that they interact with the composition of morphisms similarly as pseudofunctors.

-/

@[expose] public section

universe w v' u' v v₁ v₂ v₃ v₄ u₁ u₂ u₃ u₄ u

# Mathlib Analogist Report

## Mode
api-alignment

## Slug
ts229glue

## Iteration
229

## Question
(1) Does Mathlib already provide morphism-gluing for `SheafOfModules`/`PresheafOfModules`
(a `homOfLocalCompat`-shaped lemma / hom-sheaf)? (2) Is `presheafHom (…) (…)`-as-`TopCat.Sheaf`
+ `existsUnique_gluing` the right idiom for "global morphism = global section of the
internal-hom sheaf", or is `sheafHomSectionsEquiv`/`Sheaf.homEquiv` preferred? (3) Is the
`localSection` `naturality` (transport along `(Uᵢ).ι ''ᵁ ((Uᵢ).ι ⁻¹ᵁ V) = V`) the
Mathlib-idiomatic way, or another Mathlib-absent coherence gap of the kind that killed
the C-bridge?

## Verdicts (summary)

| Decision | Verdict | Severity |
|---|---|---|
| Q1. Module-level morphism-gluing / hom-sheaf exists in Mathlib | NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL | informational |
| Q1′. Ab/Type-level gluing engine reusable (`presheafHom`, `sheafHomSectionsEquiv`) | PROCEED (reuse) | informational |
| Q2. `presheafHom` + `existsUnique_gluing` is the right idiom | PROCEED (with `sheafHomSectionsEquiv` finish) | informational |
| Q3. `localSection` naturality (slice ↔ open-immersion) | NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL — same gap-class as the dead C-bridge | **high** |

## Headline (the data the ROUTE-C PAUSE decision needs)

**The A-bridge `homOfLocalCompat` and the dead C-bridge `dual_isLocallyTrivial` are blocked
on the SAME root Mathlib-absent infrastructure**, and that infrastructure is a *named,
documented Mathlib TODO*: `Mathlib/Topology/Sheaves/Over.lean:19-22`. Mathlib ships the bare
1-categorical equivalence `TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U : Over U ≌ Opens ↥U`
(`Topology/Sheaves/Over.lean:41`) but explicitly leaves undone the upgrade the project keeps
hitting:

> "show that both functors of the equivalence `overEquivalence U` are continuous and induce
> an equivalence between `Sheaf ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) A` and
> `Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology U) A` for any category `A`."

This is exactly the "open-immersion slice-site equivalence ~150–300 LOC" the iter-228 prover
hit on the C-bridge ([[ts228-cbridge-slice-blocker]]). The A-bridge's `localSection` is a
*section-direction slice* of the very same comparison.

**Consequence for the dispatch:** `exists_tensorObj_inverse` (L2143) needs BOTH the A-bridge
AND the C-bridge (its own body comment, L2151-2160). They share the same gap. Therefore:

- Dispatching a prover at the A-engine **in isolation closes nothing route-relevant**: even
  a successful `homOfLocalCompat` leaves the C-bridge `dual_isLocallyTrivial` blocked on the
  identical slice gap, so the residual `sorry` does not move. This is precisely the
  "cost estimate by inspection, falsified by prover" trap — the A-bridge looks independent but
  is the same wall wearing a different hat.
- The genuinely route-unblocking move is to **build the shared general bridge once** (the
  Mathlib TODO: `Sheaf (J.over U) A ≌ Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology U) A` + the
  module-pullback/`restrictFunctorIsoPullback` compatibility). That single build unblocks A
  *and* C, and is a clean upstream-PR candidate (completing a documented TODO). Realistic
  size ~200–350 LOC.

So the honest framing for the USER: the A-engine is **not** a fresh, independent ~120-190 LOC
win. It is one face of a ~200–350 LOC shared-infrastructure build that is the real precondition
for the whole `exists_tensorObj_inverse` route.

## Major

### Q1 — Mathlib has NO module-level morphism-gluing or hom-sheaf

Confirmed by directory sweep of `Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/{Sheaf,Presheaf}/*`: there is no
`internalHom`, `sheafHom`, or `homOfLocalCompat` for `PresheafOfModules`/`SheafOfModules`
(matches [[ts228-cbridge-slice-blocker]]'s loogle finding). What Mathlib *does* have:

- **Type/Ab-level hom-sheaf** (`CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafHom.lean`, general site `(C,J)`,
  values in any `A`): `presheafHom F G` (L46), `presheafHomSectionsEquiv` (L80),
  `Presheaf.IsSheaf.hom` (L207, needs `G` a sheaf), `sheafHom` (L236), `sheafHomSectionsEquiv`
  (L241). This is the engine the project's route 1–3 names — and it is the right one.
- **SheafOfModules slice machinery** (`Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Sheaf/PushforwardContinuous.lean`):
  `SheafOfModules.over M X` (L53) = restriction over the **slice** `Over X` (= `pushforward (𝟙)`),
  `Hom.over` (L58), `pushforwardOver`/`overPushforwardOverAdj` (L267/L274),
  `pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence` (L305); and `QuasicoherentData.bind`
  (`Quasicoherent.lean:358`) — **object/descent-data gluing** over a site cover `J.CoversTop X`.

Critically, *every* SheafOfModules-native restriction/gluing in Mathlib is built on the
**slice** restriction `over X` and the site cover `CoversTop`, **never** the open-immersion
topological pullback the project uses (`M.restrict (Uᵢ).ι` via `Scheme.Modules.pullback` /
`restrictFunctorIsoPullback`). The project chose open-immersion restriction (forced by
`IsLocallyTrivial`, `tensorObj_restrict_iso`); Mathlib's gluing world is slice-indexed. The
bridge between them is the absent piece. Verdict: NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL for the module layer;
PROCEED-by-reuse on the Ab/Type engine + the existing `Scheme.Modules.homMk` (L2103) to
re-attach `𝒪_X`-linearity.

### Q2 — `presheafHom` + `existsUnique_gluing` is correct; finish with `sheafHomSectionsEquiv`

`presheafHom (M.val.presheaf) (N.val.presheaf)` is a `Cᵒᵖ ⥤ Type`, a sheaf via
`Presheaf.IsSheaf.hom` (needs `N.val` a sheaf — it is). Packaged as `TopCat.Sheaf (Type) X`,
`existsUnique_gluing` (`Topology/Sheaves/SheafCondition/UniqueGluing.lean:180`) applies: its
instance side-conditions (`HasLimitsOfSize Type`, `forget` reflects/preserves limits) are
trivially met for `Type`. So the **cover→⊤** gluing step is `existsUnique_gluing` over the
cover `U` with `⨆ U = ⊤`.

The **⊤→morphism** step is *not* a hand-unfold of the `⊤`-section: use
`presheafHomSectionsEquiv` (L80) / `sheafHomSectionsEquiv` (L241). For `Opens X`, `⊤` is
terminal, so `(presheafHom F G).sections ≅ presheafHom(F,G).obj (op ⊤)` and the equiv lands
the glued section as an honest `F ⟶ G`. This avoids re-deriving the terminal-slice cleanup by
hand. Recommended idiom: `existsUnique_gluing` (cover→⊤) ∘ `presheafHomSectionsEquiv` (⊤→hom)
∘ `Scheme.Modules.homMk` (linearity). All three are Mathlib/already-built; no parallel API.
Verdict: PROCEED.

### Q3 — `localSection` naturality IS the same Mathlib-absent gap that killed the C-bridge

`presheafHom F G` indexes its sections at `Uᵢ` over the **slice** `Over Uᵢ`
(`(Over.forget Uᵢ).op ⋙ F ⟶ (Over.forget Uᵢ).op ⋙ G`, `SheafHom.lean:46`). The project's
local datum `fᵢ : M.restrict (Uᵢ).ι ⟶ N.restrict (Uᵢ).ι` lives over the **open-immersion
pullback** (`Opens ↥Uᵢ`). Converting `fᵢ` into a section of `presheafHom` over `Uᵢ` — the
`localSection` construction, with the `eqToHom`-conjugation along `(Uᵢ).ι ''ᵁ ((Uᵢ).ι ⁻¹ᵁ V) = V`
— is the **section-direction realization of the un-done `Topology/Sheaves/Over.lean` TODO**.
It is the same gap-class as the C-bridge's `(pushforward β)(dual A) ≅ dual(pushforward β A)`
([[ts228-cbridge-slice-blocker]]): both are the open-immersion ↔ slice sheaf comparison Mathlib
leaves unbuilt.

Mathlib supplies the algebraic backbone but **no helper that discharges the naturality
automatically**:
- `overEquivalence : Over U ≌ Opens ↥U` (`Topology/Sheaves/Over.lean:41`) — bare 1-categorical;
- `Opens.isOpenEmbedding_obj_top : U.isOpenEmbedding.functor.obj ⊤ = U` (`TopCat/Opens.lean:391`);
- `U.isOpenEmbedding.functor.obj ((Opens.map U.inclusion').obj V) = V ⊓ U`
  (`TopCat/Opens.lean:433`) and its `≤U` corollary at L444 — the exact `''ᵁ⁻¹ᵁ = V` identity.

The `localSection.naturality` must hand-assemble these into a coherence proof; it is real
`eqToHom`/`Over.map` work, not a discharged idiom. **It is a genuine Mathlib-absent gap.**

**Size, frankly:** the A-bridge instance is *smaller* than the C-bridge's because it needs only
the **section** direction (one section per `i` + its single naturality field + the overlap
`compat` translation + the `⊤`→hom cleanup), not a full natural iso of internal-hom functors in
two arguments with an inverse. Estimate **~80–150 LOC for `homOfLocalCompat` alone**, on top of
the gluing assembly — **not** the 30–60 LOC the plan hopes, and the plan's ~120–190 LOC total is
only achievable if the slice transport goes unusually smoothly (it did not for the C-bridge). And
per the Headline, the C-bridge's ~150–300 LOC slice transport is *still required separately*
unless the shared general bridge is built once.

## Informational

- The project's open-immersion-restriction formulation is the root cost driver. Had the local
  data been stated with Mathlib's native `SheafOfModules.over (Uᵢ)` (slice) + `J.CoversTop`,
  the hom-sheaf `presheafHom` (also slice-indexed) would align directly and much of
  `pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence` / `QuasicoherentData.bind` could be reused. This is a
  DIVERGE_INTENTIONALLY already locked in upstream (`IsLocallyTrivial`/`tensorObj_restrict_iso`
  use open-immersion `restrict`); flagging it only so the planner knows the bridge is the price
  of that earlier choice, not a fresh surprise.
- `existsUnique_gluing`'s side-conditions are free for `Type`; no instance hunting needed.

## Persistent file
Not written — this dispatch granted write access to the report path only ("Your only writable
target is the report path above"). The design rationale is inlined above; if the planner wants
it under `analogies/ts229glue.md`, re-dispatch with `--write-domain 'analogies/**'`.

Overall verdict: PROCEED on the `presheafHom`+`existsUnique_gluing`+`sheafHomSectionsEquiv`+`homMk`
idiom (it is the right and only Mathlib engine), but the `localSection` naturality is a real
Mathlib-absent coherence gap of the SAME class that killed the C-bridge (the documented
`Topology/Sheaves/Over.lean` slice-site TODO); building `homOfLocalCompat` alone (~80–150 LOC)
does NOT close `exists_tensorObj_inverse` because the C-bridge shares the gap — the
route-unblocking move is the single shared general bridge (~200–350 LOC, completes a Mathlib TODO).

**Bottom line for the ROUTE-C PAUSE decision:**

1. **Q1** — Mathlib has *no* module-level morphism-gluing / hom-sheaf. It has the Ab/Type engine (`presheafHom`, `presheafHomSectionsEquiv`, `Presheaf.IsSheaf.hom`, `sheafHomSectionsEquiv` in `Sites/SheafHom.lean`) and SheafOfModules slice machinery (`SheafOfModules.over`/`Hom.over`, `QuasicoherentData.bind`) — but the latter is *all* slice-+-`CoversTop`-based, never the open-immersion pullback the project uses. Reuse the Ab engine + existing `homMk`; the module-morphism-gluing layer must be built.

2. **Q2** — `presheafHom`-as-`TopCat.Sheaf` + `existsUnique_gluing` (cover→⊤) is the right idiom; finish ⊤→morphism with `presheafHomSectionsEquiv`/`sheafHomSectionsEquiv` rather than hand-unfolding. PROCEED.

3. **Q3 — the high-severity finding:** the `localSection` naturality is a *genuine Mathlib-absent gap of the same class that killed the C-bridge*. Both are the open-immersion ↔ slice-site sheaf comparison that Mathlib leaves as a **named TODO** (`Topology/Sheaves/Over.lean:19-22`; only the bare `overEquivalence : Over U ≌ Opens U` is shipped). The A-bridge needs only the section-direction slice (~80–150 LOC, **not** the plan's 30–60), but **`exists_tensorObj_inverse` needs both A and C**, which share this gap. So a prover dispatched at the A-engine alone closes nothing route-relevant — it's the same wall. The route-unblocking move is to build the shared general bridge once (~200–350 LOC, completing the Mathlib TODO), which frees A and C together.
